cs:emotipets:hero.title: EMOTIpets.
cs:emotipets:hero.description: A children's brand built around emotional literacy — plush characters, packaging, and play that give kids a vocabulary for how they feel.

cs:emotipets:sections.heading: Overview
cs:emotipets:sections.body:
Inspired by my passion for design, EMOTIpets is a children's brand centered on emotional intelligence — drawing from my experience as an older sister, observing and entertaining younger siblings, and understanding how kids communicate feelings.

From visiting toy departments to designing concept packaging, my goal was to bring EMOTIpets to life on shelves — creating awareness of emotional connection for children supported by thoughtful, joyful storytelling.

cs:emotipets:sections.heading: Mood Board
cs:emotipets:sections.body: Drawing from existing children's IP that nails emotional resonance — soft forms, saturated palettes, expressive faces, and tactile appeal.

cs:emotipets:sections.heading: Logo Exploration
cs:emotipets:sections.body: Exploring type treatments, colour combinations, and lockups that balance playfulness with legibility across product and packaging contexts.

cs:emotipets:sections.heading: Form Development
cs:emotipets:sections.body: Building out the character family — iterating on silhouette, proportion, and expression to make each EMOTIpet instantly readable as a distinct emotional archetype.

cs:emotipets:sections.heading: Bringing it to shelves.
cs:emotipets:sections.body: Structural and graphic packaging design that communicates the emotional theme of each character at a glance — from hangtag to box.

cs:emotipets:sections.text: Giving kids a soft, squeezable way to name what they feel — that's the whole brief.
cs:emotipets:sections.author: Design intent
